summary: Today's sweep leans toward Democracy / press freedom, Sci-fi / horror, AI / computing. The strongest items in this run are Middle East crisis live: US war on Iran has cost around $29bn, Pentagon says, Amazon’s ultrafast 30-minute deliveries are now available in more cities, and Amazon employees are "tokenmaxxing" due to pressure to use AI tools.

mainItems.title: Middle East crisis live: US war on Iran has cost around $29bn, Pentagon says
mainItems.summary: Pete Hegseth and other officials appear before House to face grilling on Iran war expenditure and military operations Iran has expanded its definition of the strait of Hormuz into a “vast operational area” far wider than before the war,…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: Amazon’s ultrafast 30-minute deliveries are now available in more cities
mainItems.summary: Following a limited test run in parts of Seattle and Philadelphia that started last December, Amazon Now is officially available to millions of Amazon shoppers across the United States.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: Amazon employees are "tokenmaxxing" due to pressure to use AI tools
mainItems.summary: Workers are using an internal AI tool to automate non-essential tasks.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to the shape of current AI systems and where the tooling is moving.

mainItems.title: Canvas owner reaches ‘agreement’ with hackers to secure stolen data
mainItems.summary: Instructure, the company behind the Canvas learning management platform, says it has "reached an agreement" with hackers that breached its systems last week to prevent stolen data from being leaked online.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, student agency, and school policy.

mainItems.title: Battle Zombified Sheep in The Country in Co-op PvE game ‘Flock Off!’ [Trailer]
mainItems.summary: New Zealand has Black Sheep, and soon Scotland will have Flock Off!, the debut title from Bunkhouse Games and publisher Shoreline Games.
mainItems.whyItMatters: On-theme for your cultural interests, especially when the concept is strong.

mainItems.title: ICE Agents Have List of 20 Million People on Their iPhones Thanks to Palantir
mainItems.summary: The comments made by a senior ICE official at a trade show highlight how Palantir is increasing the speed at which ICE operates. Most people detained by ICE have no criminal conviction.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: ‘Phasmophobia by Alan Wake’ Collaboration Event On Now [Trailer]

mainItems.title: Trump’s sudden cut in substance testing is ‘going to kill people’, experts warn
mainItems.summary: Samhsa said funding cannot be used to purchase or distribute fentanyl test strips or other drug test kits The Trump administration ’s decision to restrict use of federal funds for fentanyl test strips, in what officials described as a…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: Forza Horizon 6 leaking early was "not the result of a pre-load issue", developers Playground say, while threatening "franchise-wide and hardware bans"
mainItems.summary: Following news of Forza Horizon 6 's PC version leaking online ahead of release , developers Playground Games have said the leak was wasn't the result of a Steam pre-load issue.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth your time if you care about design, criticism, and not just hype.

mainItems.title: Paying in sweat! How Debbie Allen went from stardom in Fame to conquer Hollywood
mainItems.summary: She played the world’s coolest dance teacher and has had big success as an actor, director and choreographer, winning a Golden Globe, Emmys and an Olivier. Now, she is back on Broadway.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth watching for authoritarian drift, selective enforcement, and democratic norms.

mainItems.title: Opinion: We Should Pay More for the Best Teachers
mainItems.summary: A version of this essay appeared on Matthew Yglesias’ Slow Boring, a site dedicated to offering pragmatic takes on politics and public policy.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, classroom practice, and educational judgment.

mainItems.title: Health experts say cruise ship hantavirus outbreak isn't a risk to public at large
mainItems.summary: Passengers who were potentially exposed to hantavirus on a cruise are back home and being monitored for symptoms, as scientists maintain that risk to the public is low.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

mainItems.title: The clipping economy: How short-form video 'clippers' are overrunning the internet
mainItems.summary: Short-form clips of long interviews and shows are taking over the internet. But behind the sea of social media clips are marketplaces offering freelance clippers money per view.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Interesting where engineering, science, and tech infrastructure collide.

new.title: Scavengers Reign
new.why: Animated speculative sci-fi with strong worldbuilding and a weirder imagination than most prestige SF.

new.title: Sugar
new.why: Noir detective structure with a genre wrinkle and a sensibility that feels adjacent to your crime/strangeness overlap.

new.title: The Lazarus Project
new.why: Time-loop espionage, moral pressure, and speculative plotting — very plausible fit.

new.title: Bodies
new.why: Time-spanning conspiracy/mystery structure that fits your taste for long-arc puzzle storytelling.

new.title: Constellation
new.why: A little more cerebral and mood-heavy, but it scratches the identity / reality-slippage itch.

new.title: The Devil’s Hour
new.why: Dark, twisty, and structurally ambitious without feeling like homework.

older.title: Counterpart
older.why: Probably the single cleanest “James show you may have missed”: espionage + parallel-world sci-fi + adult competence.

older.title: Patriot
older.why: Dry, strange, melancholy spy storytelling with a very distinct voice.

older.title: Utopia
older.why: Paranoid conspiracy energy, formal boldness, and a willingness to get strange and ugly.

older.title: Rubicon
older.why: A low-key conspiracy thriller that leans heavily on intelligence work, paranoia, and institutional atmosphere.

older.title: Continuum
older.why: A cleaner old-school sci-fi fit if you want procedural momentum wrapped around time-travel politics.

older.title: Person of Interest
older.why: Starts procedural and then quietly becomes one of the smartest AI / surveillance shows on television.

shared.title: Bad Sisters
shared.why: Darkly funny, well-observed, and ensemble-driven in a way that plausibly overlaps with Hacks / Derry Girls / Mare of Easttown energy.

shared.title: Detectorists
shared.why: Gentle, funny, humane, and specific — a softer shared-watch possibility.

shared.title: Astrid et Raphaëlle
shared.why: French procedural with a strong central duo and exactly the kind of foreign-crime appeal Maggie may share with you.

shared.title: Somebody Somewhere
shared.why: A warmer character-driven recommendation if the shared overlap leans more Bear / Good Place than pure detective work.

highlights.title: 12 unexpected sourdough bread recipes that aren't crusty loaves: From sourdough flatbread to sourdough milk bread — and even sourdough babka.
highlights.notes: Pinned from today's briefing, the kind of bread-nerd rabbit hole worth keeping.
